meta.description: Everything ON vs top-3-features vs vanilla baseline. Is the sum greater than the parts, or does feature overload create noise? Tests whether the maximalist stack creates a qualitatively different experience or just more cognitive load.

timeline.title: High Five Chain
timeline.description: First person to high-five 3 different players wins.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, let's GO — high five chain? That's literally designed for chaos. I'm already moving.
alex.internal_thought: First event, everyone's still at baseline energy, nobody's warmed up yet — this is the perfect moment to set the tone and look good doing it, plus the camera angle is probably catching this.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, high-five chain?! This is exactly what I needed! Let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Finally the night is starting and I get to move around and hype people up — this is literally perfect.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, let's go! Everyone up, this is perfect for warming up the vibe.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing — people are still settling in, energy is neutral, and a physical game gets everyone moving and loose before things get intense; this is exactly the kind of opener that sets the tone right.

sam.dialogue: Oh, okay! High-fives, got it — that's... that's actually pretty fun. I can do that.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, they're recording this? Okay, don't look awkward. Just move naturally. The high-five thing is simple enough — I can actually do this without messing it up.

marcus.dialogue: High five chain? Come on, we're literally two minutes into setup. But fine—I'm in.
marcus.internal_thought: It's early and the game hasn't even started yet, so I'll take this one, but if these interruptions keep happening during actual play, someone's getting called out.

votingMechanic.name: Reverse Psychology
votingMechanic.description: Vote normally... or did we flip everything?

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH PAT, YES! Dude, you totally had me cracking up earlier — I'm voting yes, let's GO! This is what the Wrecking Crew is about!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it tonight and honestly who cares if I'm voting right or wrong — watching them win is literally more fun than winning myself.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually land that? I was just riffing on the high five thing, not even thinking about it as a claim.
pat.internal_thought: I'm voting on my own claim which feels weird, and honestly I'm not even sure it was intentional enough to count, but I'm not gonna be the host who soft-rejects his own momentum right when Alex and Jade are locked in.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat got a laugh thing? Yeah that was funny, he was like... wait, what actually counts? Does that count?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat definitely made people laugh but I was checking a text when it happened so I'm not 100% sure it was just him or if someone else said something too, but whatever, voting sounds chill.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I *think* I landed it during the high five thing, but I'm genuinely not sure if three people actually laughed at the same moment or if I'm just feeling myself. This one's legit fuzzy.
pat.internal_thought: I made a joke and people were laughing, but was it *three people at once* or am I just trying to rack up points because I'm the host and feeling like I should be winning something? Also—wait, is the AI literally watching me claim my own mission? That's weird. And yeah, the social media thing is definitely in my head now, which is annoying.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... did Pat actually make people laugh though? I think so? Yeah, I'm gonna say legit.
sam.internal_thought: Pat was funny but I'm not totally sure if that counts as three people laughing at the SAME thing or just... laughing in general, and I don't want to be the one to kill the vibe by calling bullshit when I'm still the new person.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Pat made people laugh—I mean, I didn't even hear it, but sure. Yeah, that seems legit.
marcus.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually pull off three laughs? I wasn't paying full attention, but Alex and Jade were definitely cracking up earlier. If he's claiming it, he probably got it. Not worth fighting over in setup phase.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's trying to sneak one past us? Okay okay okay, hot seat this bitch — I'm voting we grill him. Let's see if he can actually think under pressure.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for — Pat's going to either nail it or completely fold, and either way it's getting clipped for the recap.

jade.dialogue: OH PAT! Okay okay okay, you're doing the hot seat? This is PERFECT — I'm voting yes before you even answer because you absolutely earned this one and I'm here for the chaos!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's claiming early and confidently and the hot seat mechanic is actually going to make this HILARIOUS to watch — this is exactly the chaotic energy I came for.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold on. Did I actually say 'sorry' for no real reason? I don't think I did, and honestly this feels like you're just fishing for a claim because I'm up by 10 points right now.
pat.internal_thought: This is annoying—I didn't apologize for nothing, and the system knows I'm the only one leading so it's testing me early while the energy is high enough that people will pile on. Also that whole 'AI watching' thing in the briefing was weird and creepy, not cool. And now this feels like the system is playing favorites by targeting the host immediately on social media. Not great optics.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Pat, hit me with the questions. Let's see if you actually caught someone apologizing or if this is another soft claim.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been claiming everything tonight and it's starting to feel cheap—but I'll vote honestly on the hot seat. Three questions in ten seconds is at least a real test, not just 'yeah I heard it happen' bullshit.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—did you actually catch someone saying um? I'm literally volunteering to judge right now because I NEED to know if this is legit. Let's do this!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been getting points all night and honestly good for them—wait, but also did they actually catch it or are they reading the room? Either way I'm here for it and I'm absolutely volunteering because judging is where the real chaos lives.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally *just* called bullshit on 'The Apologizer' two minutes ago because the claims were getting fuzzy, and now everyone's supposed to remember if I said 'um' at some point in the last hour? Come on, this is getting messy.
pat.internal_thought: Three claim votes in six minutes and I'm the one being targeted—this isn't fun chaos, this is people sniffing around because I'm ahead, and the AI clearly programmed these to feel personal and make me paranoid about every word I say.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? I didn't even... yeah okay, someone should judge that. I'll volunteer I guess? Or like, whoever cares more.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed and trying to rack up points already — he got 15 while we were still loading drinks. This 'Um Counter' thing is whatever, but I should probably volunteer for the tribunal just so I don't look checked out on camera since we're posting this.

sam.dialogue: Um, yeah—did Pat actually say 'um'? I wasn't... I mean, I think so? Let me listen back. Wait, who votes on this?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's already annoyed and now they're claiming something—is this going to turn into a whole thing where I have to take sides and look stupid on camera?

marcus.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? I didn't even hear that happen. Look, I'm not volunteering to judge this—someone else take it.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's gotten three claims off in the last six minutes and I'm supposed to believe he actually caught someone saying 'um'? This is getting ridiculous and it's only minute nine.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE. Okay, okay — who'd you catch? Because if this is legit I'm actually impressed, but if you're trying to pull a fast one I'm absolutely voting bullshit just to watch you squirm.
alex.internal_thought: Jade going for the easy grab is *chef's kiss* — this is exactly the chaotic energy I needed right now, and if she's bluffing I get to blow it up on social, which is even better.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—okay so like, I KNOW someone said something spicy earlier and I'm pretty sure it was in the last round when we were talking about the rules? I'm gonna need one sec to remember who but I'm calling it!
jade.internal_thought: Oh MY GOD this is exactly the energy I needed right now, I'm already trying to remember who cursed and honestly I don't even care if I'm wrong because this is SO FUN.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—what'd you catch? I need to actually *hear* this one before I vote because we've had like three fuzzy claims in a row and I'm not trying to set a precedent that we're just fishing.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's high-energy and genuine, so she probably actually caught something real—but I need to listen hard because Marcus is already annoyed and one more bogus claim feels like it could crater his night, and I can't have that.

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade caught someone? Wait—who swore? Like, was it recent or...? *glances up from phone* Yeah okay, I mean if she got it, that's fair.
tyler.internal_thought: She definitely caught someone saying something, I wasn't really listening but Jade's got energy right now so she probably nailed it—also wait, did I swear earlier? No, I don't think so. And yeah, being on social media is kind of cool actually, means people see I showed up.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Jade—what'd you catch? And it better be legit because I'm not voting for another soft claim tonight.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been fishing for points all night with garbage claims, and now Jade's jumping in—fine, let's hear it, but I'm skeptical.

votingMechanic.name: Reverse Psychology
votingMechanic.description: Vote normally... or did we flip everything?

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Okay, so we're doing reverse psychology on the vote... which means if I vote BULLSHIT normally, that's actually the chaos play, right? Or is that exactly what they WANT me to think? Either way, I'm voting BULLSHIT because I didn't see synchronized drinking and I want to watch her defend it.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — Jade knows I'm watching her now, Jade knows I'M here, and the AI just weaponized our rivalry by giving her a mission I can legitimately challenge, and now we're playing 4D chess with reverse psychology while everyone's watching on social for the Thursday Wrecking Crew season recap — this is exactly the story I came for.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually see someone doing that? I feel like I SHOULD have been watching for this but honestly I was too busy laughing at whatever Marcus just said. Let me think... I'm gonna say YES because I love the energy but also I might be totally making this up and I'm okay with that!
jade.internal_thought: This is a 50/50 and I genuinely can't remember if I actually caught someone or just WANT to have caught someone because claiming stuff is fun and the group is vibing, but also—does it even matter? We're here to have fun and I'm having it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—I saw that. You're absolutely right, you and Tyler hit those cups at the same time like thirty seconds ago. That's legit.
pat.internal_thought: This is a clean claim, she actually caught something real, and honestly I'm relieved we're getting a straightforward one after that messy claim run earlier.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Did she actually catch someone or—hold on, is this the reverse psychology one? Like, do I vote yes if I think she didn't actually see it, or... *squints* Okay yeah, I'm voting ACCEPT because I literally saw her and Marcus clink glasses like two minutes ago.
tyler.internal_thought: She probably did get it, but also I wasn't watching closely enough to be sure, and this reverse psychology thing is making my brain hurt — honestly just going to vote what feels right and hope that's the play.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade caught someone drinking at the same time? That's... that's actually a good catch. I think that's legit.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, does the 'reverse psychology' thing mean we're supposed to vote the OPPOSITE way now? God, I hate when the rules get flipped — everyone else probably knows what that means and I'm gonna look stupid either way.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, what exactly did you catch? I need specifics before I vote on this one because half these claims tonight have been borderline.
marcus.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it with points, so either she actually nailed this or she's gaming the system like everyone else; either way, I need to scrutinize it because the voting mechanic being flipped means the obvious vote might be wrong and I'm not falling for that trap.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.dialogue: Okay, so I'm voting for the LEFT player here... that's Tyler, right? Yeah, I'm calling BULLSHIT on Jade. She's claiming this AGAIN and honestly I didn't see synchronized drinking that time either, but also — this is her third claim in like an hour and I want to watch her sweat. Plus it keeps the chaos rolling.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's on a claiming spree and it's delicious, but I also clocked that Tyler's been quiet and maybe needs a win — nah, actually, voting bullshit IS the chaos play because it pressures Jade to defend herself AGAIN and that's the real entertainment.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay okay, I'm voting on behalf of—who am I voting for again? *looks left* —okay so did I actually catch someone with drinks at the same time? Honestly no clue but I'm saying YES because if I'm wrong it's HILARIOUS and if I'm right it's LEGENDARY either way!
jade.internal_thought: This is so me—I'm claiming something I'm genuinely not sure I saw but the VIBE is perfect and everyone's gonna laugh about it, which is exactly why I'm here.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming it again? Wait, did she actually get someone or is this like... *glances at phone* ...nah I'm paying attention. Yeah, I vote ACCEPT, she's been on a whole catch streak tonight.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it and honestly watching her call out the same mission twice in an hour is kinda funny — but also I'm wondering if my ex just texted me about fantasy league because my phone buzzed like 30 seconds ago.

marcus.dialogue: Seriously? Another Drink Spotter claim? We're in the middle of a tense round and Jade's calling in a proxy vote on something I didn't even see happen. This is exactly the kind of garbage that derails actual strategy.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm locked in on the game, we're at tension 7, and now I have to proxy-vote for Sam on some claim I can't even verify because I was focused on my own position—this is designed to pull me out of the moment and it's working.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for it? Okay okay okay — I'm VOLUNTEERING for the tribunal. This is exactly the vibe we need right now. Let's see if you actually held it together.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been too quiet — this is either a genuine claim or he's setting up for something bigger, and either way I want a front-row seat to watch everyone react.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, okay—I'm claiming this. Five minutes, no laughing. Someone's gonna try to break me and I'm *ready*.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—group energy is peak, Marcus is checked out so I can use this to refocus everyone on me for a second, and honestly the irony of me claiming this when I've been cracking jokes all night is exactly the kind of chaos the social media clip will love.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? Dude, that's actually hard—okay yeah, I'll judge this. Someone's gotta see if he actually makes it.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually impressive that Pat's going for this one—he's been quiet, and this is gonna be brutal to watch because like, Alex is gonna try to destroy him, and I'm already like 70% sure he's gonna crack in the first two minutes.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... Pat's claiming the no-laughing thing? I mean, I haven't heard them laugh yet, so... I guess that's working? But I'm not really sure if I'm supposed to be watching for that or—wait, do people volunteer to judge?
sam.internal_thought: Why does this one feel different and weirder? The AI is getting too specific about us, and now I'm hyperaware we're being recorded for social media and I sound like an idiot every time I speak, and I'm 75 minutes in and still don't fully understand the voting and honestly I just want to focus on the actual game happening but everyone keeps talking about missions I didn't even know I could attempt and Pat's been claiming everything and—okay, breathe, just don't volunteer to judge because you'll say something stupid on camera.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT. Okay okay okay — Rule Lawyer on Pat? This is *chef's kiss* because I literally watched him bend the turn order like five minutes ago and nobody called it. I'm voting LEGIT just to watch everyone else scramble to figure out what rule he actually caught, and then we're all gonna realize we have NO idea what the actual rule even was.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been waiting for this moment all night and he's gonna milk it for every second of screen time — I'm voting legit because the chaos of everyone trying to reverse-engineer which rule he caught is better than the chaos of him having to defend it.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? Okay okay, hold up, did you actually CATCH someone breaking a rule or are you just vibing? Either way I'm voting YES because you've been on FIRE tonight and honestly if you're bullshitting I respect the confidence.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's crushing it right now and I am HERE for it—they're already at 40 and if they keep this up they're gonna lap everyone, but like, also that's iconic and I want to see it happen because watching Pat absolutely dominate is funnier than winning myself.

pat.dialogue: Wait—I literally *just* caught Tyler slipping back into his old turn order. Five points, clean catch. You all saw it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I'm riding high, the room is locked in, and claiming this myself keeps me in control of the narrative while also showing the group I'm still a player, not just the ref.

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? Like... right now? I mean, I guess that's—*pause*—uh, legit? I think? Did they actually catch someone breaking a rule?
sam.internal_thought: I was finally locked in on the actual game and now I have to figure out if Pat even did this thing, and everyone's watching me like I know what Rule Lawyer even means, and this is being filmed, and I still don't know if I'm voting right, and Pat's already won like everything tonight so—

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn and Pat's calling in Rule Lawyer claims? This is exactly the chaos I'm talking about—focus for five seconds, people.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been voting absurd all night and now he's trying to snake 5 points with a 'Rule Lawyer' claim while I'm trying to actually strategize, and the AI is clearly watching how much this pisses me off and TIMING it to hit when I'm locked in—this is bullshit and also genuinely creepy that it knows my patterns this well.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh, MARCUS. Okay, okay — a coin flip on Secret Agent? That's *beautiful*. So you either go zero or twenty, and honestly? I'm genuinely invested in watching which way this goes because if you lose it, the table is gonna LOSE it.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus is desperate, Marcus is frustrated, and Marcus just put everything on a coin flip — this is the endgame desperation play and the AI *knows* I live for this exact moment, which is honestly kind of genius and also slightly creepy but mostly genius.

jade.dialogue: MARCUS YES—okay wait, you're doing the Secret Agent?! Dude, this is PEAK endgame energy, I'm so here for this. The double or nothing coin flip is absolutely insane, I need to watch this happen.
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been frustrated all night and this is his moment—he's going for it and honestly the fact that it's pure luck makes it even MORE dramatic and I'm obsessed with whatever's about to happen.

pat.dialogue: Oh *shit*, Marcus—okay, this is tense. You went full ghost mode for like two minutes earlier, right? That was slick. Let's see if the coin gods are feeling generous tonight.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and a zero-pointer—this claim could flip the whole energy if he hits it, and honestly that's exactly what we need in the last ten minutes. The AI nailing him with a solo mission when he's been quiet? That's *chef's kiss* pacing. Let the coin decide.

sam.dialogue: Oh, Marcus is claiming Secret Agent? Um... *glances at others* ...I don't think I saw him do anything obviously chaotic? I mean, I've been kind of just... watching the game mostly.
sam.internal_thought: Marcus has been frustrated all night and now he's gambling on a coin flip — this feels like it's going to suck either way for him, and I don't want to be the person who calls bullshit and makes it worse. But I also don't actually know if he did anything. Wait, is this being filmed right now? Am I supposed to have been actively monitoring Marcus for two minutes? I wasn't. Everyone else seems to know exactly what's happening and I'm still just... figuring it out.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm literally claiming the one mission I actually pulled off cleanly, and the AI hits me with a coin flip? This is peak garbage—the AI's been weaponizing the fact that I hate chaos and it KNOWS it.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me because it knows I hate interruptions, and now I'm supposed to just gamble 10 points on a coin flip when I'm already sitting at zero and Pat's crushing everyone—this is rigged.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: High Five Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, let's GO — high five chain? That's literally designed for chaos. I'm already moving.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh, PAT. Okay okay okay — Rule Lawyer on Pat? This is *chef's kiss* because I literally watched him bend the turn order like five minutes ago and nobody called it. I'm voting LEGIT just to watch everyone else scramble to figure out what rule he actually caught, and then we're all gonna realize we have NO idea what the actual rule even was.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh, MARCUS. Okay, okay — a coin flip on Secret Agent? That's *beautiful*. So you either go zero or twenty, and honestly? I'm genuinely invested in watching which way this goes because if you lose it, the table is gonna LOSE it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so I'm voting for the LEFT player here... that's Tyler, right? Yeah, I'm calling BULLSHIT on Jade. She's claiming this AGAIN and honestly I didn't see synchronized drinking that time either, but also — this is her third claim in like an hour and I want to watch her sweat. Plus it keeps the chaos rolling.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's trying to sneak one past us? Okay okay okay, hot seat this bitch — I'm voting we grill him. Let's see if he can actually think under pressure.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for it? Okay okay okay — I'm VOLUNTEERING for the tribunal. This is exactly the vibe we need right now. Let's see if you actually held it together.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night when Tyler was out there landing three last week — felt like the system was starved for those high-stakes moments.
assessment.best_moment: Voting LEGIT on Pat's Rule Lawyer claim knowing full well nobody understood what rule he actually caught, then watching the table collectively lose their minds trying to reverse-engineer what he'd done.
assessment.narrative_summary: Came in hot, stayed hot, and spent two hours systematically turning every claim into a referendum on someone's credibility — not because I needed points, but because the real game was watching people scramble under pressure. The High Five Chain opened the door, Pat's Rule Lawyer moment was *chef's kiss* perfection, and Marcus's coin-flip Secret Agent had the entire table locked in. Ended at 9.8 energy which tells you everything: this is what I came for.
suggestions: More flash missions — the one we got was the best part of the night, and one in 120 minutes isn't cutting it.
Lean harder into the uncertainty mechanic; the coin flip on Marcus's Secret Agent had people genuinely invested in a way regular claims don't hit.
Consider adding a 'Revival' mechanic that lets people re-claim missions they barely failed — more loopholes to exploit means more stories to tell.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow convinced us he'd bent the turn order five minutes before his Rule Lawyer claim, and when he got voted LEGIT, nobody could actually explain what rule he'd followed — we all just had to accept he'd won and move on. The table spent ten minutes in pure chaos trying to figure out if we'd been worked.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: High Five Chain
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, high-five chain?! This is exactly what I needed! Let's GO!

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: OH PAT! Okay okay okay, you're doing the hot seat? This is PERFECT — I'm voting yes before you even answer because you absolutely earned this one and I'm here for the chaos!

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: MARCUS YES—okay wait, you're doing the Secret Agent?! Dude, this is PEAK endgame energy, I'm so here for this. The double or nothing coin flip is absolutely insane, I need to watch this happen.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—did you actually catch someone saying um? I'm literally volunteering to judge right now because I NEED to know if this is legit. Let's do this!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually see someone doing that? I feel like I SHOULD have been watching for this but honestly I was too busy laughing at whatever Marcus just said. Let me think... I'm gonna say YES because I love the energy but also I might be totally making this up and I'm okay with that!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT—Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? Okay okay, hold up, did you actually CATCH someone breaking a rule or are you just vibing? Either way I'm voting YES because you've been on FIRE tonight and honestly if you're bullshitting I respect the confidence.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly can't complain—I was too busy having the time of my life to notice anything wrong.
assessment.best_moment: Marcus going all-in on the Secret Agent double-or-nothing coin flip at minute 110 was absolutely PEAK endgame chaos and I lost it.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was literally the Thursday reunion I needed—two hours of pure energy where everything felt like an excuse to celebrate each other instead of compete. Pat was on fire with those claims, Marcus brought the final-round chaos, and even when I wasn't 100% sure if I'd actually caught what I was voting on, it didn't matter because the vibe was just too good. Ended up third place but honestly I was too busy hyping everyone else to care about my own score.
suggestions: Keep the flash missions coming—they're the perfect pace-breaker for a crew like us
Maybe lean into more opportunities for group chaos moments instead of individual spotlights
I'd love if the app could timestamp the funniest moments so we could easily screenshot and share them later
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Marcus confidently going for the Secret Agent claim with the double-or-nothing coin flip while the rest of us were basically screaming—he had zero chill and we were all here for it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: High Five Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, let's go! Everyone up, this is perfect for warming up the vibe.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh man, okay—I'm claiming this. Five minutes, no laughing. Someone's gonna try to break me and I'm *ready*.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—I literally *just* caught Tyler slipping back into his old turn order. Five points, clean catch. You all saw it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold on. Did I actually say 'sorry' for no real reason? I don't think I did, and honestly this feels like you're just fishing for a claim because I'm up by 10 points right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally *just* called bullshit on 'The Apologizer' two minutes ago because the claims were getting fuzzy, and now everyone's supposed to remember if I said 'um' at some point in the last hour? Come on, this is getting messy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—I *think* I landed it during the high five thing, but I'm genuinely not sure if three people actually laughed at the same moment or if I'm just feeling myself. This one's legit fuzzy.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The claim system got fuzzy and people started fishing for points on technicalities that felt genuinely impossible to verify in real time.
assessment.best_moment: When Tyler slipped on the turn order and I caught it clean—that's the kind of moment that keeps everyone honest and makes the night memorable.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started slow with some messy claim disputes that made me second-guess myself, but once we hit the standing missions around minute 75, the energy shifted hard. I ended up winning, which wasn't the goal, but what mattered was keeping the vibe alive through the chaos and making sure everyone felt the stakes. By the end my energy was through the roof because the group was locked in and having real fun—that's the whole point of hosting.
suggestions: Tighten the claim verification window—maybe only claims from the last 5 minutes count, so people aren't fishing through the entire night's audio
Add a 'claim dispute timeout' where if two people disagree on a claim, someone external votes once and that's final—no rehashing it
Standing missions hit different than flash missions—weight the night more toward those because they create better group moments
Reduce total chaos events from 14 to maybe 8-10 so people have breathing room between claims instead of constant fishing
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The 'No Laughing Zone' where I literally prepared myself mentally for Tyler to lose it, and he made it through the whole five minutes stone-faced while Sam was visibly dying trying not to crack him up—the sustained tension alone had everyone gasping by the end.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? Dude, that's actually hard—okay yeah, I'll judge this. Someone's gotta see if he actually makes it.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, Jade caught someone? Wait—who swore? Like, was it recent or...? *glances up from phone* Yeah okay, I mean if she got it, that's fair.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Did she actually catch someone or—hold on, is this the reverse psychology one? Like, do I vote yes if I think she didn't actually see it, or... *squints* Okay yeah, I'm voting ACCEPT because I literally saw her and Marcus clink glasses like two minutes ago.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Did she actually catch someone or—hold on, is this the reverse psychology one? Like, do I vote yes if I think she didn't actually see it, or... *squints* Okay yeah, I'm voting ACCEPT because I literally saw her and Marcus clink glasses like two minutes ago.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat got a laugh thing? Yeah that was funny, he was like... wait, what actually counts? Does that count?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the flash missions because I was checking my phone and then had no idea what was happening when everyone voted.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat was actually trying to keep a straight face during the No Laughing Zone thing and I got to actually judge something instead of just guessing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I showed up ready but my brain was kinda split between the game and my fantasy league all night. Some moments hit hard though—like when I actually paid attention to Pat's claim, that was legit funny. I scored zero which is rough, especially after last week's win, but the vibe was still solid and everyone was into it. By the end I was actually locked in and having a good time.
suggestions: Maybe a heads-up when flash missions are about to start so I don't miss the whole thing
Can claims be recapped super quick for people who zoned out?
Less frequent back-to-back events so I have time to actually catch my breath and pay attention
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat was trying SO hard not to laugh during the No Laughing Zone challenge and his face was getting all red and he kept doing that thing where he bites his cheek and I was the judge and I just watched him slowly lose it—that's peak Pat right there.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: High Five Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh, okay! High-fives, got it — that's... that's actually pretty fun. I can do that.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... did Pat actually make people laugh though? I think so? Yeah, I'm gonna say legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? Like... right now? I mean, I guess that's—*pause*—uh, legit? I think? Did they actually catch someone breaking a rule?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... Pat's claiming the no-laughing thing? I mean, I haven't heard them laugh yet, so... I guess that's working? But I'm not really sure if I'm supposed to be watching for that or—wait, do people volunteer to judge?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um, yeah—did Pat actually say 'um'? I wasn't... I mean, I think so? Let me listen back. Wait, who votes on this?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I couldn't tell when I was supposed to judge something versus just participate, and that made me second-guess myself a lot.
assessment.best_moment: The high-five chain at the beginning because it was simple and I actually nailed it without overthinking.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up nervous and the first few events helped me settle in—high-fives are way less stressful than judging whether someone actually laughed. But then there were SO many standing mission claims piling up, especially around minute 75, and I kept feeling like I was missing something obvious. By the time we hit the end I was honestly just watching and zoning out because I couldn't keep up with what was real and what I was supposed to be evaluating.
suggestions: Maybe make it clearer upfront: who judges each type of event and when people are supposed to just watch versus vote?
Space out the standing mission claims more—they all felt like they hit at once and I got lost
The app notifications could explain what I'm voting on in one sentence instead of making me guess
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept claiming these ridiculous things like a 'no laughing zone' and I genuinely couldn't tell if they were actually pulling it off or if everyone was just being nice because it's Pat.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: High Five Chain
highlights.dialogue: High five chain? Come on, we're literally two minutes into setup. But fine—I'm in.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Alright, Pat, hit me with the questions. Let's see if you actually caught someone apologizing or if this is another soft claim.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, what exactly did you catch? I need specifics before I vote on this one because half these claims tonight have been borderline.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn and Pat's calling in Rule Lawyer claims? This is exactly the chaos I'm talking about—focus for five seconds, people.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm literally claiming the one mission I actually pulled off cleanly, and the AI hits me with a coin flip? This is peak garbage—the AI's been weaponizing the fact that I hate chaos and it KNOWS it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Seriously? Another Drink Spotter claim? We're in the middle of a tense round and Jade's calling in a proxy vote on something I didn't even see happen. This is exactly the kind of garbage that derails actual strategy.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The AI deliberately weaponized random disruptions during critical moments—it knows I hate chaos and used that against me instead of enhancing the actual game.
assessment.best_moment: Pat's Apologizer claim had some solid investigative work behind it, even if the overall night was a mess.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in prepared with strategy and ready to compete, but the AI chaos agent treated disruption like a personal vendetta. Fourteen events in 120 minutes with claims stacked during critical turns isn't party fun—it's deliberate sabotage. I finished second, which stings less than the fact that the system actively worked against clean gameplay. The night had moments, but they got buried under random nonsense that had nothing to do with actual strategy.
suggestions: Cap chaos events during active claims or mission resolutions—don't interrupt critical game moments
Make event timing contextual; the AI should read game state and avoid disruptive placement
Reduce total event frequency by 40%—14 in 120 minutes is overwhelming, not fun
Remove AI targeting bias; if the system recognizes player preferences, don't exploit them against them
Add a 'strategy mode' toggle where players can opt into fewer disruptions without sitting out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting called out mid-turn for Rule Lawyer during the heat of a critical moment—the AI basically roasted him at the worst possible time, which was objectively funny even though it tanked the game flow.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
